issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on html
issuesFound.explanation: The root html element sets scroll-behavior: smooth, but the focus indicator is globally suppressed on interactive elements via the :focus-visible rule on line 345 which sets outline: none without a visible replacement in that specific block. This prevents keyboard users from identifying the active element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the default focus outline for non-keyboard interactions without providing any visual feedback. While intended for mouse users, it can block users with certain assistive technologies that simulate mouse clicks but require visual focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 352: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 1px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The high-contrast media query removes the outline on line 358. In forced-colors mode, background-colors and box-shadows are often stripped, making the outline the only reliable way to see focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 358: outline: 2px solid ButtonText;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button base class removes the outline. Although transitions are defined, there is no box-shadow or border-color change defined within this specific focus-visible block to ensure visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 585: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative like a border or shadow, making it impossible for keyboard users to track focus on the brand link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 685: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle removes the focus outline. Since this is a critical navigation element, keyboard users will lose their place when attempting to open the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 716: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the outline. The negative outline-offset provided on line 779 does nothing because the outline width is effectively zero.
issuesFound.fix: Line 778: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the outline on focus. While a box-shadow is provided on line 811, it uses a semi-transparent blue which may not meet visibility requirements against all backgrounds.
issuesFound.fix: Line 808: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0, completely stripping the focus indicator for keyboard users on the search field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 815: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submission button removes the focus outline without a visible replacement, hindering keyboard navigation within the search form.

issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline. Without a replacement style, users cannot visually confirm which account action is selected.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature "Learn more" links remove the outline. Since these links use a pseudo-element to cover the whole card, the lack of focus indicator makes the entire card's focus state invisible.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class has a min-height of 32px. While it meets the 24px absolute minimum, it fails the 44px AAA recommendation and may be difficult to hit on mobile devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle is sized at 28px, which is significantly smaller than the recommended 44px target for primary navigation controls.

issuesFound.explanation: The search input height is set to 28px, which is a small target for mobile users and users with tremors.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto. This fails to visually hide the content while keeping it in the accessibility tree; it effectively makes the content visible or breaks the "hidden" intent.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition (translateY) to slide into view. This motion can cause vestibular distress for sensitive users and lacks an override in the media query on line 443.
issuesFound.fix: Line 444: .skip-link { transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards use a transform: translateY(-2px) on hover. While a media query exists on line 1028, it only targets the hover state and not the base transition defined on line 1021.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1029: .feature-card { transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards animate a transform on hover. The reduced-motion block on line 1253 only removes the hover transform but leaves the transition active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1254: .product-card { transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word spacing causes words to run together, significantly hindering readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 215: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em. Tightening the space between characters reduces legibility, especially at smaller font sizes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 216: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em). Large headings with negative tracking are harder to read for users with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 908: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.32:1
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary variable is set to #aaaaaa on a white background (#ffffff). This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 75: --colour-text-secondary: #707070;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #ee9999 / background #ffffff ratio 2.45:1
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-error variable (#ee9999) used for error text and markers against white backgrounds fails the minimum contrast ratio.
issuesFound.fix: Line 89: --colour-error: #b91c1c;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global 'a' selector removes text-decoration. In blocks of text (like .prose or paragraphs), links will only be distinguishable by colour, which violates WCAG 1.4.1 for users with color blindness.

issuesFound.explanation: The root font-size is set to 12px. This makes the base text size for the entire document too small for comfortable reading, forcing users to zoom immediately.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the focus indicator for any element that does not match the :focus-visible heuristic. If a browser's heuristic fails or a user requires a focus ring on mouse click for orientation, the indicator is entirely absent.
issuesFound.fix: Line 422: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 1px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 434: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component explicitly sets outline: none. While it has a transition for background-color, it does not provide a high-contrast focus ring (like a box-shadow or border) within this rule to replace the native outline.

issuesFound.fix: Line 701: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons are explicitly sized to 20px by 20px. This is a significant failure of target size requirements for touch and pointer users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 709: min-width: 24px; Line 710: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class has a min-height of 32px. While it has padding, the total height does not reach the 44px recommended for AA (WCAG 2.5.5) or the 24px minimum (WCAG 2.5.8) without guaranteed surrounding spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 624: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input height is set to 28px. This is a small target for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 854: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search button is sized at 28px by 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation for interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 891: width: 44px; Line 892: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: Filter buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is difficult to target on mobile devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1288: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, failing to meet the 44px target size recommendation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1461: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition (translateY) to slide into view. There is no override in the prefers-reduced-motion block to disable this movement for sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 525: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: translateY(0); } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes a transition for 'transform'. This causes movement on hover/active states that is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 640: transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast);

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards use a transform transition for a lift effect on hover. While there is a partial override on line 1116, it only targets the hover state and not the base transition property.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1105: transition: border-color var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base);

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards use a transform transition for movement on hover. The reduced motion block on line 1350 only removes the hover transform but leaves the transition active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1341: transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base);

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on html
issuesFound.explanation: The root html element sets scroll-behavior: smooth, but the focus indicator is globally suppressed on interactive elements via the :focus-visible rule on line 415 which sets outline: none without a visible replacement in that specific block. This prevents keyboard users from identifying their location on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 415: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the focus indicator for any element that does not match the :focus-visible heuristic. If a browser's heuristic fails or a user requires a focus ring on mouse click for orientation, the indicator is entirely absent.
issuesFound.fix: Line 422: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 1px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility removes the outline but only provides an outline-offset. Since the outline itself is set to none in the preceding global block, the offset applies to a zero-width line, rendering no visible indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 434: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component explicitly sets outline: none. While it has a transition for background-color, it does not provide a high-contrast focus ring (like a box-shadow or border) within this rule to replace the native outline.
issuesFound.fix: Line 645: .btn:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link removes the outline without providing a box-shadow or border replacement, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the logo is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 735: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus outline. Without a visible replacement, keyboard users cannot navigate the mobile header effectively.
issuesFound.fix: Line 768: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the outline and only set an offset. This results in no visible focus indicator for items within submenus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 838: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the outline. While it adds a box-shadow, the shadow uses a semi-transparent blue (rgba) which may disappear in high-contrast modes or against certain backgrounds, and the outline: none is not safely replaced for all AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 873: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0, effectively killing the focus ring for the search input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative like a box-shadow or border.
issuesFound.fix: Line 905: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline. The border-radius is adjusted, but no visible ring is added to replace the removed outline.
issuesFound.fix: Line 931: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the focus indicator. Since these links often wrap the entire card via a pseudo-element, the lack of a focus ring makes the entire card's focus state invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Product filter buttons remove the focus indicator, preventing keyboard users from knowing which category filter they are currently highlighting.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1311: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons remove the focus indicator without a replacement, breaking keyboard navigation for plan selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1485: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table region is focusable (tabindex="0") but removes its focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see that the region is focused for scrolling.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion triggers remove the focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot identify which question they are about to expand.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1768: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the outline. The provided box-shadow uses a semi-transparent color (rgba) which is insufficient as a primary focus indicator in many environments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1818: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 1819: .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on mobile nav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class on the drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not receive feedback that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1545: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');`
       Line 1561: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer missing aria-hidden toggle
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened or closed, the `aria-hidden` attribute is not updated on the drawer element. This can lead to the drawer content remaining in the accessibility tree while visually hidden or being ignored when visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1545: `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`
       Line 1561: `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function for dropdown menus ensures `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` are set but does not initialize `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. Assistive technologies may not correctly identify the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1745: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function for dropdown menus removes the `hidden` attribute and adds a CSS class but fails to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button to `true`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1663: `inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not update aria-expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function for dropdown menus removes the active CSS class but fails to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button to `false`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1676: `inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Sticky header does not update aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onScroll` function hides the header visually using the `header--hidden` class when scrolling down but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. Keyboard users navigating backwards may accidentally land on links within a hidden header.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1835: `_header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`
       Line 1840: `_header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button missing aria-hidden toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `_update` function for the scroll-to-top button toggles the `hidden` attribute but does not manage `aria-hidden`. This can cause the button to remain detectable by screen readers while it is transitioning or visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1925: `_btn.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`
       Line 1930: `_btn.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list missing aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_open` and `_close` functions for the site search do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the search input (`combobox`). Screen reader users will not know if the suggestion list is currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2215: `_inputEl.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');`
       Line 2225: `_inputEl.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list missing aria-hidden toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `show` and `hide` functions in `suggestionListRenderer` toggle the `hidden` attribute on the listbox but do not update `aria-hidden`.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2125: `_listEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`
       Line 2132: `_listEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-selected update
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates` function updates the `is-active` CSS class on filter buttons but does not update the `aria-selected` attribute. Since these act as tabs, the selected state must be programmatically exposed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2515: `btn.setAttribute('aria-selected', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: View mode toggle buttons missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function updates the `aria-pressed` attribute on the buttons but the initial state is not set in an `init` function, leading to a lack of state communication on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2615: `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => b.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode)));`

issuesFound.title: Pricing billing toggle missing aria-checked update
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState` function updates the `is-checked` class on the toggle track but does not update the `aria-checked` attribute on the `_toggleEl` (which has `role="switch"`).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2895: `_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-checked', String(isYear));`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function for FAQ items animates the panel and updates CSS classes but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3145: `item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not update aria-expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function for FAQ items animates the panel and updates CSS classes but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3175: `item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing aria-hidden toggle on panel
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ `open` and `close` functions toggle the `hidden` attribute but do not manage `aria-hidden` on the panel, which can lead to inconsistent behavior in some screen reader/browser combinations during animation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3145: `item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`
       Line 3175: `item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function for the carousel does not set the initial `aria-pressed` state on the `_playBtn` based on the `_playing` variable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3515: `if (_playBtn) _playBtn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(_playing));`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in `inlineValidator` applies CSS classes and updates error text but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3915: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', String(!result.valid));`

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard missing aria-hidden toggle on panels
issuesFound.explanation: The `_goToStep` function in the wizard toggles the `hidden` attribute and `aria-hidden` on the leaving/entering panels, but the initial state for all steps except the first is not explicitly set to `aria-hidden="true"` in the `init` function.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4265: `step.panelEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: File upload zone missing aria-busy update
issuesFound.explanation: The `_uploadEntry` function tracks progress but does not set `aria-busy="true"` on the entry row or the zone while an upload is in progress.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4415: `entry.rowEl.setAttribute('aria-busy', 'true');`
       Line 4430: `entry.rowEl.removeAttribute('aria-busy');`

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher buttons missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function for the theme switcher applies the theme but does not initialize the `aria-pressed` state on the toggle buttons to match the loaded preference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5055: `qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === _current)));`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on mobile nav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class on the drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not receive feedback that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1545: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');`
       Line 1561: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer missing aria-hidden toggle
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened or closed, the `aria-hidden` attribute is not updated on the drawer element. This can lead to the drawer content remaining in the accessibility tree while visually hidden or being ignored when visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1545: `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`
       Line 1561: `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function for dropdown menus ensures `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` are set but does not initialize `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. Assistive technologies may not correctly identify the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1745: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function for dropdown menus removes the `hidden` attribute and adds a CSS class but fails to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button to `true`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1663: `inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not update aria-expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function for dropdown menus removes the active CSS class but fails to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button to `false`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1676: `inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Sticky header does not update aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onScroll` function hides the header visually using the `header--hidden` class when scrolling down but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. Keyboard users navigating backwards may accidentally land on links within a hidden header.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1835: `_header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`
       Line 1840: `_header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button missing aria-hidden toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `_update` function for the scroll-to-top button toggles the `hidden` attribute but does not manage `aria-hidden`. This can cause the button to remain detectable by screen readers while it is transitioning or visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1925: `_btn.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`
       Line 1930: `_btn.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list missing aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_open` and `_close` functions for the site search do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the search input (`combobox`). Screen reader users will not know if the suggestion list is currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2215: `_inputEl.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');`
       Line 2225: `_inputEl.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list missing aria-hidden toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `show` and `hide` functions in `suggestionListRenderer` toggle the `hidden` attribute on the listbox but do not update `aria-hidden`.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2125: `_listEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`
       Line 2132: `_listEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-selected update
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates` function updates the `is-active` CSS class on filter buttons but does not update the `aria-selected` attribute. Since these act as tabs, the selected state must be programmatically exposed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2515: `btn.setAttribute('aria-selected', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: View mode toggle buttons missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function updates the `aria-pressed` attribute on the buttons but the initial state is not set in an `init` function, leading to a lack of state communication on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2615: `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => b.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode)));`

issuesFound.title: Pricing billing toggle missing aria-checked update
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState` function updates the `is-checked` class on the toggle track but does not update the `aria-checked` attribute on the `_toggleEl` (which has `role="switch"`).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2895: `_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-checked', String(isYear));`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function for FAQ items animates the panel and updates CSS classes but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3145: `item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not update aria-expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function for FAQ items animates the panel and updates CSS classes but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3175: `item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing aria-hidden toggle on panel
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ `open` and `close` functions toggle the `hidden` attribute but do not manage `aria-hidden` on the panel, which can lead to inconsistent behavior in some screen reader/browser combinations during animation.

       Line 3175: `item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function for the carousel does not set the initial `aria-pressed` state on the `_playBtn` based on the `_playing` variable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3515: `if (_playBtn) _playBtn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(_playing));`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid

issuesFound.fix: Line 3915: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', String(!result.valid));`

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard missing aria-hidden toggle on panels
issuesFound.explanation: The `_goToStep` function in the wizard toggles the `hidden` attribute and `aria-hidden` on the leaving/entering panels, but the initial state for all steps except the first is not explicitly set to `aria-hidden="true"` in the `init` function.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4265: `step.panelEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: File upload zone missing aria-busy update
issuesFound.explanation: The `_uploadEntry` function tracks progress but does not set `aria-busy="true"` on the entry row or the zone while an upload is in progress.
issuesFound.fix:

       Line 4430: `entry.rowEl.removeAttribute('aria-busy');`

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher buttons missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function for the theme switcher applies the theme but does not initialize the `aria-pressed` state on the toggle buttons to match the loaded preference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5055: `qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === _current)));`

issuesFound.fix: Line 1015: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}
issuesFound.explanation: The search icon (🔍) inside the submit button is decorative as the button already has a visually hidden label "Submit search". It should be hidden from assistive technology to avoid redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1161: 🔍

"true" : "false"} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer's open and close functions do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive the current expanded/collapsed state of the navigation menu, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.open function, add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); In the mobileNav.close function, add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) do not have their aria-pressed state updated when selected. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is currently active, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In the _syncToggleState function, add: setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product count changes, the update is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users with visual impairments may not know the results have updated.
issuesFound.fix: In the applyFilters function, after _updateCountBadge, add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch results update missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When search results are fetched and rendered, the number of suggestions is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users with visual impairments may not know the search results are available.
issuesFound.fix: In the _fetchAndRender function, after setting _searchState.suggestions, add: liveRegion.announce(`${results.length} suggestion${results.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} available`);

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed of the expanded state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2080: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`. Line 2090: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands an FAQ answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not notified of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product count and grid but does not announce the change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the updated results.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied.');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates displayed prices but does not announce the billing period change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the updated prices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: after `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields as invalid with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen reader users are not alerted to the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3700: in the invalid branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`. In the valid branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button when the drawer is initially closed. Screen reader users do not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function does not set aria-expanded="false" on dropdown trigger buttons when panels are initially hidden. Screen reader users do not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2220: after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the search input when the suggestion listbox is initially hidden. Screen reader users do not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2780: after `_initialised = true;`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the navigation drawer by removing a CSS class but does not set aria-hidden="true". The drawer remains in the accessibility tree when invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2090: after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In the open function, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel by setting the hidden attribute but does not set aria-hidden="true". The panel remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2260: after `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In _openInstance, add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion listbox not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._close function hides the suggestion listbox but does not set aria-hidden="true". The listbox remains in the accessibility tree when invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2810: after `suggestionListRenderer.hide();`, add `setAttr(suggestionListRenderer._listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In _open, add `setAttr(suggestionListRenderer._listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` after `suggestionListRenderer.show();`.

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._update function hides the button by setting the hidden attribute but does not set aria-hidden="true". The button remains in the accessibility tree when invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: after `_btn.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(_btn, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. When showing, add `setAttr(_btn, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` after `_btn.removeAttribute('hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: Click event listener on non-button element without keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The delegate() function attaches click events to elements matching a selector, but if the target is a non-interactive element (like a div), there is no corresponding keydown handler for Enter/Space. This makes the control inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200: function delegate(parent, event, childSelector, handler, opts) { ... if (event === 'click') { parent.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { const match = closest(e.target, childSelector, parent); if (match) handler.call(match, e, match); } }); } ... }

issuesFound.title: Icon-only interactive element missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component uses an IconButton without an aria-label prop. Icon-only buttons require an accessible name; without it, screen reader users hear only "button" with no purpose.

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not set aria-invalid. Screen reader users are not informed when the field has an error state, missing critical validation feedback.

issuesFound.title: Required-field marker announces bare asterisk to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component's required marker is a bare asterisk without aria-hidden. Screen readers may announce "asterisk" or "star" without context, creating noise for users.

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component has an isLoading state but does not set aria-busy. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is in a busy/loading state, missing important status feedback.

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component's current page link lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify the current page link within navigation, reducing wayfinding efficiency.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles the dropdown submenu lacks an aria-expanded attribute. Screen reader users cannot know if the submenu is currently open or closed, which hinders navigation.

issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component for theme toggling is missing its required aria-label prop. Icon-only buttons without an accessible name are unusable for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle buttons use only visual styling to indicate the active state. Screen reader users are not informed which filter is selected, degrading the experience.

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current in DesktopNav
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the desktop navigation lacks an aria-current="page" attribute. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot easily identify the current page.

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current in MobileNav
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile navigation lacks an aria-current="page" attribute. Screen reader users cannot efficiently identify their current location within the site.

issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component does not set the aria-invalid attribute when the invalid prop is true. Screen reader users are not alerted to form field errors, preventing correction.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1141: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` (inside open), Line 1158: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._updateCountBadge` function updates the product count text, but there is no accompanying write to an ARIA live region. Users are not notified when the number of filtered results changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1348: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count));`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds a CSS class and inserts an error message for invalid fields, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"`. Screen readers will not identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1848: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function removes the error class for valid fields but does not explicitly set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute. The field may remain marked as invalid to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1844: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function sets up the trigger but does not initialize `aria-expanded="false"`. Users will not know the initial state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1188: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` trigger is initialized without an `aria-controls` attribute linking it to the drawer's ID. This breaks the programmatic relationship between the button and the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1188: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function removes the visible class from the overlay but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer or overlay immediately. The elements may remain discoverable by screen readers while visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1158: `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing._onToggleClick` function switches the billing period but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the toggle switch.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1588: `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(next === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function changes the visible state of the navigation drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1047: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` (add this line inside the `open` function)

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._onViewToggle` function changes the grid/list view mode but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1058: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` (add before `addClass(_drawer, ...)`), Line 1074: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (add before `removeClass(_drawer, ...)`).

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._onViewToggle` function changes the view mode between 'grid' and 'list' but only updates `aria-pressed` for the buttons. The logic is correct, but the `_onViewToggle` function is triggered via delegation and does not ensure the state is synced for all buttons if the DOM changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1435: `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { setAttr(b, 'aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === mode)); });` (Ensure this is called consistently after state change).

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._updateCountBadge` function updates the text content of the product count badge, but this element is not a live region. Users of assistive technology will not be notified when the number of filtered products changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1368: `_countBadge.setAttribute('role', 'status');` (add during `productFilter.init` for the `_countBadge` element).

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds a CSS class `CSS.fieldInvalid` and updates the error message, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field. This prevents screen readers from identifying the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1845: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` (add inside the `else` block of `_renderResult`).

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function removes the invalid CSS class when a field becomes valid, but it does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1841: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` (add inside the `if (result.valid)` block of `_renderResult`).

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu.init` function registers dropdowns but does not explicitly set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger buttons for those that are closed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1154: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (add inside the `register` function).

issuesFound.title: toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register`, the code checks for `aria-controls` and sets it if missing, but the `mobileNav.init` function (Line 1096) does not ensure the `_trigger` is linked to the `_drawer` via `aria-controls`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1096: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));` (add inside `mobileNav.init`).

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function removes the `CSS.open` class and hides the overlay, but it does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the `_drawer` element. The drawer remains discoverable by screen readers even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1074: `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` (add inside `mobileNav.close`).

issuesFound.fix: Line 1068: `<button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>`

issuesFound.title: active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link is visually marked as current via a class, but lacks `aria-current="page"`. Screen reader users are not notified that this link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The `Button` component handles a loading state but does not apply `aria-busy="true"`. Assistive technology will not be notified that the button is currently processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button controls the navigation drawer (`#nav-menu`), but no `aria‑controls` attribute points to the drawer’s `id`, leaving the relationship undisclosed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
After obtaining the drawer element, add  
```js
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);
```  
so the button references the controlled region.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: In `openInstance()` and `closeInstance()` the dropdown panel is shown/hidden, but the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` state is never updated, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside each function add  
```js
setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(inst.open));
```  
to keep the attribute in sync with the panel’s visibility.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria‑pressed (two‑state toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger acts as a two‑state toggle (open/closed) but never receives an `aria‑pressed` update, which is required for button‑like toggles.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling the panel, also set  
```js
setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(inst.open));
```  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are expanded/collapsed via `open(item)` and `close(item)`, yet the question button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never changed, so screen readers cannot perceive the state.
issuesFound.fix:
In both `open()` and `close()` add  
```js
setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(item.open));
```  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria‑pressed (toggle button)
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ question button is a toggle but does not receive `aria‑pressed`, violating the ARIA button pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the open state, also set  
```js
setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(item.open));
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestions panel is shown/hidden by setting `_searchState.open`, but the `<input>` never receives an `aria‑expanded` change, leaving the combobox state ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_open()` and `_close()` add  
```js
setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', String(_searchState.open));
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle (`[data-billing-toggle]`) switches between monthly and annual plans but never updates `aria‑pressed`, so AT cannot convey its state.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling the period, add  
```js
setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear));
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Each period button (`[data-period]`) acts as a mutually exclusive toggle but does not set `aria‑pressed`, breaking the ARIA tab pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_syncToggleState()` after determining the active button, set  
```js
setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel period tabs missing aria‑selected
issuesFound.explanation: The period tabs in the carousel are given the `active` class but never receive `aria‑selected`, so screen readers cannot identify the selected tab.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_syncToggleState()` (or equivalent) add  
```js
setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button missing aria‑label (accessible name)
issuesFound.explanation: The “scroll‑to‑top” button (`#scroll-top-btn`) has no accessible name, making it invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an `aria-label` attribute, e.g.  
```js
setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone missing aria‑label (accessible name)
issuesFound.explanation: The drag‑and‑drop upload zone does not provide an accessible name, so users of assistive technology cannot discover its purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
When binding the zone, add  
```js
setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-label', zoneEl.getAttribute('data-upload-label') || 'Upload files');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates (price change on billing period) not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, price numbers are animated but no ARIA live region is updated, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the new values.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating all price displays in `setPeriod()`, add  
```js
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingUpdated || 'Pricing updated for selected billing period');
```  
or update a dedicated live‑region element with the new price summary.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑navigation “hamburger” button (`_trigger`) opens and closes the off‑canvas drawer, but the script never toggles its `aria‑expanded` attribute. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the navigation is currently expanded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The same hamburger button acts as a two‑state toggle (open / closed) but never reflects its state via `aria‑pressed`, which is required for toggle buttons that are not native checkboxes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed the script only adds a CSS class and the `hidden` attribute. Because the element remains in the accessibility tree, assistive technologies may still expose it.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown’s button (`trigger`) toggles the panel visibility, yet the code never sets `aria‑expanded` to reflect the open/closed state, breaking the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads the dropdown buttons have no `aria‑expanded` attribute, so the initial state is ambiguous to assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panels hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown only adds a CSS class and the `hidden` attribute; the panel remains in the accessibility tree because `aria‑hidden` is not set.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not update aria‑expanded on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion toggles answer panels, but the script never changes the `aria‑expanded` state on the question button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the current state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the FAQ list is rendered, the question buttons have no `aria‑expanded` attribute, so the default state is undefined for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: FAQ answer panels hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The panels are hidden with the `hidden` attribute only; without `aria‑hidden` they remain perceivable to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not update aria‑expanded when suggestions open/close
issuesFound.explanation: The search box shows a suggestion list, but the input never receives an `aria‑expanded` value, breaking the combobox pattern.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the search input has no `aria‑expanded` attribute, so its default state is ambiguous.

issuesFound.title: suggestion list hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `<ul>` that holds autocomplete suggestions is hidden via the `hidden` attribute only; without `aria‑hidden` it stays in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters change, the visible count badge is updated, but no live‑region announcement is made, so users of assistive technology cannot hear the new result count.

issuesFound.title: form validation errors are not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator adds error messages but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the offending field, so assistive technologies cannot convey the error state.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period switch (monthly / annual) visually indicates the state but never updates `aria‑pressed`, leaving screen‑reader users without state information.

issuesFound.title: scroll‑to‑top button hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The “back‑to‑top” control is hidden via the `hidden` attribute only; without `aria‑hidden` it remains in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: suggestion list does not have role="listbox" and aria‑expanded on the controlling input (duplicate of Issue 10, but also missing role)
issuesFound.explanation: The autocomplete list is created with `role="listbox"` but the controlling input never receives `aria‑expanded`, breaking the combobox pattern. (See Issue 10 for the fix; also ensure `role="combobox"` on the input.)

fpTitles: dropdownMenu panels hidden without aria‑hidden
FAQ accordion items do not update aria‑expanded on open/close
FAQ accordion items missing initial aria‑expanded
FAQ answer panels hidden without aria‑hidden
siteSearch input missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
suggestion list does not have role="listbox" and aria‑expanded on the controlling input (duplicate of Issue 10, but also missing role)

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button (SELECTORS.navToggle). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in mobileNav.close add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); or toggle the attribute based on the new state.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger (dropdown menus)
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance in dropdownMenu adds/removes the “open” class on the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving the expanded state undisclosed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After inst.open = true; add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in _closeInstance add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger (search suggestion list)
issuesFound.explanation: suggestionListRenderer.mount sets aria‑autocomplete, aria‑controls, autocomplete, role but never sets aria‑expanded on the search <input>. The listbox visibility is toggled via hidden attribute only, so screen readers cannot perceive the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In mount after setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox'); add setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); and toggle it to true when the list is shown and back to false when hidden.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria‑pressed (billing‑period toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle element (SELECTORS.toggle) is given role="switch" but its pressed state is only reflected by CSS classes; aria‑pressed is never set, so the current period (monthly/annual) is invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod after updating the UI, call setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false'); or update aria‑pressed whenever the toggle state changes.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate uses srUtils.markInvalid which adds aria‑describedby but never adds aria‑invalid to the field. Users of assistive technology therefore receive no programmatic indication that a field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In srUtils.markInvalid add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); and in srUtils.markValid remove the attribute or set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing initial aria‑expanded (attribute not set on page load) – dropdown menus
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are given aria‑haspopup but never receive an initial aria‑expanded="false" value, violating the requirement that the expanded state be explicitly defined on load.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.init or when registering a trigger, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); and ensure aria‑controls points to the panel.

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing initial aria‑expanded (attribute not set on page load) – mobile navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav button (SELECTORS.navToggle) receives no aria‑expanded value on initial render, leaving its state undefined for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init after locating _navToggle, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); and set aria‑controls to the drawer’s id.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing initial aria‑expanded (attribute not set on page load)
issuesFound.explanation: The search <input> is given aria‑autocomplete and aria‑controls but no initial aria‑expanded value, so screen readers cannot know whether the suggestion list is currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix: In siteSearch.init or suggestionListRenderer.mount, add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); and toggle it when the list opens/closes.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑controls (attribute not set on page load) – billing‑period toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle element has no aria‑controls linking it to the price panel it shows/hides, violating the requirement that a controlling element reference its controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.init after locating _toggleEl, add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', _toggleTrack.id or another appropriate panel id).

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates not announced via live region (pricing period change)
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, price numbers are animated but no aria‑live region is updated to announce the new price to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: After _animatePlanCard updates the DOM, also set liveRegion.announce(`Pricing updated to ${price}`) or update a hidden <div role="status"> with the new price.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not updated with aria‑hidden ( scroll‑to‑top button )
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll‑to‑top button is hidden/shown by adding/removing the hidden attribute, but the code never sets aria‑hidden to true when hidden, which is required for elements that may be removed from the visual flow without hidden attribute (e.g., CSS‑only visibility toggles).
issuesFound.fix: In scrollToTop._update when !shouldShow add setAttr(_btn, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and when showing, remove the attribute or set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._updateCountBadge function updates the text content of the count badge when filters change, but does not announce this change to a live region. Users are unaware the product count has updated.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
