issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons (inst.trigger) do not have their aria-expanded attribute toggled when the panel opens or closes. The initial state is also not set in register. This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function fails to set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function sets item.open = true and shows the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where AT reports the item as collapsed even when open.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not toggle aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (_inputEl) acts as a combobox trigger but does not update aria-expanded when the suggestion list opens or closes. The initial state is also missing. AT users are not informed when the listbox is visible.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked update
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle (_toggleEl) is assigned role="switch" in init, but _syncToggleState only toggles a CSS class. It does not update aria-checked, so screen readers do not know the current state of the switch.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing._syncToggleState: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is validated as valid, markValid removes the aria-describedby reference but fails to remove or reset the aria-invalid attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technology even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markValid: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge text is updated dynamically when filters are applied, but there is no live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed that the number of products has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter._updateCountBadge: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing price updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Price values are updated dynamically when the billing period changes. While aria-label is updated, there is no live region announcement to proactively inform users of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing._updatePlanCard: liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField uses deprecated aria-dropeffect
issuesFound.explanation: The code sets aria-dropeffect="copy" and "none" on the drop zone. This attribute is deprecated in WAI-ARIA 1.1 and should be removed to avoid confusion or validation errors.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator lacks aria-live for dynamic numbers
issuesFound.explanation: The stat counter elements animate their text content dynamically. Without aria-live="polite" on the element, screen readers may not announce the counting animation or the final value effectively.
issuesFound.fix: Line statCounterAnimator.init: el.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');

fpTitles: fileUploadField uses deprecated aria-dropeffect

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions toggle CSS classes and scroll locks but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.open function: add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after addClass(_drawer, ...). Line: mobileNav.close function: add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after removeClass(_drawer, ...).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer visibility is toggled via CSS classes and the hidden attribute is not consistently managed with aria-hidden. When closed, the drawer should be removed from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.open function: add _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');. Line: mobileNav.close function: add _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu instance functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close logic updates the panel visibility but does not sync the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Assistive technology cannot detect the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: dropdownMenu._openInstance function: add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');. Line: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function: add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu instance functions do not update aria-hidden on panel
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel visibility is toggled but aria-hidden is not updated to match. Hidden panels should be removed from the accessibility tree to prevent focus traps or confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line: dropdownMenu._openInstance function: add inst.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');. Line: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function: add inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function sets the item state to open and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: faqAccordion.open function: add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after item.open = true;.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch does not manage aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox opens and closes the suggestion list but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the search input. Screen readers do not announce that the listbox is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: siteSearch._open function: add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');. Line: siteSearch._close function: add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');.

issuesFound.title: suggestionListRenderer does not manage aria-hidden on listbox
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion listbox visibility is toggled using the hidden attribute, but aria-hidden is not updated. When hidden, the listbox should be removed from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line: suggestionListRenderer.show function: add _listEl.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');. Line: suggestionListRenderer.hide function: add _listEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed or aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons update CSS classes to show the active state but do not update aria-pressed (for buttons) or aria-checked (for switches). Users relying on AT cannot identify the selected period.
issuesFound.fix: Line: pricing._syncToggleState function: inside the forEach loop, add setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));. If using _toggleEl as a switch, add setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(isYear));.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function for marking fields as invalid updates aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce validation errors immediately.
issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.markInvalid function: add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); before addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function for marking fields as valid removes the error description but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (set to "false" or removed). The field may remain announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.markValid function: add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false'); or field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge text content is updated dynamically when filters change, but there is no live region announcement. Users are not informed that the number of products has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: productFilter._updateCountBadge function: add liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`); after updating textContent.

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The savings message text is updated dynamically when the billing period changes, but no live region announcement is triggered. Users are not informed of the new savings information.
issuesFound.fix: Line: pricing._updateSavingsMessage function: add liveRegion.announce(_savingsMsg.textContent); after updating textContent.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is shown and hidden using the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but aria-hidden is not updated. When hidden, the button remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line: scrollToTop._update function: in the shouldShow block, add _btn.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');. In the !shouldShow block, add _btn.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The header is visually hidden via CSS class toggling during scroll, but aria-hidden is not updated. Screen reader users may still navigate to the hidden header content.
issuesFound.fix: Line: stickyHeader._onScroll function: when adding CSS.headerHidden, add _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');. When removing it, add _header.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');.

fpTitles: scrollToTop button visibility not synced with aria-hidden
stickyHeader visibility not synced with aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the default focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus. The outline-offset property alone does not create a visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Within the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible sets outline: none without a replacement. Users with high-contrast themes enabled lose any focus indicator, preventing them from navigating the interface.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on header logo link
issuesFound.explanation: The header logo link sets outline: none on focus-visible with only outline-offset as a replacement. This removes the visible focus ring, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the logo link is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on mobile navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle button removes the outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when this critical control is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search form submit button sets outline: none on focus-visible with no visible replacement. This prevents keyboard users from seeing when the search button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on filter tab buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons set outline: none on focus-visible with only outline-offset specified. This creates no visible focus indicator for keyboard users navigating the tab interface.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual pricing toggle buttons remove the outline on focus-visible without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which billing option button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer brand links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the outline on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when these links are focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set outline: none on focus-visible with no visible replacement. Keyboard users navigating the footer cannot see which link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs set outline: none on focus-visible with no visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when checkboxes are focused, making form navigation difficult.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon-only buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons are critical interactive elements that must have sufficient size for reliable activation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are set to 20px by 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to select these small checkboxes.

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility and may expose visually-hidden text to sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition properties but has no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this animation. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the transform animations on buttons.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transform transition and hover transform effect, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 734-737 is empty, providing no override. Users with motion sensitivity may experience discomfort from the 3D perspective transform.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below normal. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to visually merge together.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which tightens character spacing. This reduces readability for users with low vision or dyslexia, and can make text appear crowded and difficult to parse.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 325: 
```css
  letter-spacing: normal;
```

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing on headings
issuesFound.explanation: Headings (h1-h6) use letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which evaluates to -0.025em. While less severe than body text, negative letter-spacing on headings can still impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing on statistic values
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-value class uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter) which evaluates to -0.05em. Large numerical values with tight letter-spacing can be difficult to read accurately, especially for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on statistics labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% opacity white) text over a gradient background of #5588bb to #bb88ee. The resulting contrast ratio of approximately 1.74:1 fails WCAG AA requirements (4.5:1 for normal text). Users with low vision cannot read these labels.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter form labels
issuesFound.explanation: Form labels in the newsletter section use rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9) over a gradient background (#5588bb to #bb88ee), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.23:1. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (font-size: var(--font-size-sm) = 14px).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter description text
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter section paragraph uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.8) over the brand gradient background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.98:1. This is below the 4.5:1 required for normal text (16px).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter input hints
issuesFound.explanation: Input hints in the newsletter section use rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.65) over the gradient background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.61:1. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for small text (font-size: var(--font-size-xs)).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links inherit text-decoration: none and only change color on hover (from #64748b to #ffffff). Without an underline, border, or other non-color indicator, users cannot distinguish links from surrounding text except by color, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in social links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links inherit text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change on hover (from #64748b to #ffffff). Users with color vision deficiencies cannot identify these as interactive links without an additional visual indicator such as underline or border.

issuesFound.title: Root font size below 16px degrades readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, making 1rem equal to 12px. This results in body text of 12px (var(--font-size-base) = 1rem), which is below the 16px recommended minimum for readable text. Users with low vision may be unable to read the content comfortably.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration (underlines) from all links. Without additional non-colour visual differentiators such as border-bottom or font-weight changes, users with colour vision deficiencies may not distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 197: a { text-decoration: underline; } /* Or add specific underline rules for body-text links */

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, which does not create a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA).

issuesFound.title: Button component focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector explicitly removes the outline and provides no visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, failing to provide a visible focus indicator for the mobile menu button.

issuesFound.title: Submenu link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector removes outline and uses a negative outline-offset without providing a visible replacement, making focus invisible on dropdown menu items.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible rule removes outline with no visible alternative, leaving keyboard users without indication of focus on the search button.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector removes outline and provides only outline-offset and border-radius, resulting in no visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only offset and radius, failing WCAG 2.4.11 by not providing a visible focus indicator on card links.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector removes outline without providing a visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from seeing which filter tab is focused.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for the billing period toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: FAQ button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector uses outline: none with negative offset and no visible replacement, making focus invisible on accordion triggers.

issuesFound.title: Footer link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible rule removes outline without providing a visible alternative, hiding focus from keyboard users navigating footer links.

issuesFound.title: Social link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only offset and radius, failing to show visible focus on social media links.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible rule removes outline with only offset, providing no visible focus indicator for the scrollable table region.

issuesFound.title: Focus ring inset utility removes focus indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector sets only outline-offset: -3px while inheriting outline: none, resulting in no visible focus ring for elements using this utility.

issuesFound.title: Focus ring brand utility removes focus indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible selector sets only outline-color while outline remains none, leaving no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (AA). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this button.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with tremors or limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes the checkbox difficult to toggle accurately.

issuesFound.title: Primary button touch target may be below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector sets min-height: 32px, which meets the 24px minimum but falls below the 44px recommended target size. Users with motor impairments may find it difficult to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Navigation menu item touch target may be below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a and button selectors set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended target size. This affects keyboard and touch users navigating the main menu.

issuesFound.title: Search input touch target may be below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input selector sets height: 28px, below the 44px recommended target size. Users may struggle to activate the search field reliably.

issuesFound.title: Visually hidden utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which makes the content visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it available to screen readers. This fails WCAG 1.1.1 and 4.1.2 by potentially exposing hidden content or hiding it from assistive technology incorrectly.

issuesFound.title: Button motion animation lacks reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property, creating spatial movement on state changes. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override disables this animation, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Hero image motion animation lacks reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes a transform transition and hover transform that creates spatial movement. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for this selector is empty, failing to disable the animation for users who require reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Product card motion animation lacks reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card selector includes transform in its transition and hover state, creating spatial movement. No override exists in the prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable this for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card motion animation lacks reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies a transform scale effect that is not disabled in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block, potentially causing issues for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Body text negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below normal. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, violating WCAG 1.4.8 Visual Presentation (AAA).

issuesFound.title: Heading negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The h1-h6 selector sets letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which evaluates to -0.025em. Negative letter-spacing reduces readability for users with low vision or dyslexia.

issuesFound.title: Hero heading negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-content h1 selector uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter) which is -0.05em. This tight spacing can make the large heading difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Root font-size too small for readable body text
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px, making 1rem equal to 12px. Since body text uses var(--font-size-base) which is 1rem, the effective body font size is 12px, below the 16px minimum recommended for readability.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 138:
  font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Stats section label insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label selector sets color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) on a semi-transparent white background over a gradient. The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 2.5:1, below the 4.5:1 required for WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum) AA.

issuesFound.title: Global link underline removal without hover replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none globally. While some links restore underline on hover, many navigation links (main nav, account nav, footer) do not provide a non-colour visual differentiator on hover, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Main navigation link underline missing on hover
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a:hover selector changes colour but does not restore text-decoration: underline, relying solely on colour to indicate the link is interactive.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link underline missing on hover
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover selector changes colour but lacks text-decoration: underline, making it difficult for colour-blind users to identify the link state.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link underline missing on hover
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover selector changes colour without adding underline, failing to provide a non-colour indicator for users who cannot perceive the colour change.

issuesFound.title: Social link underline missing on hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:hover selector changes colour but does not restore text-decoration: underline, relying on colour alone to indicate interactivity.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded update on open
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer trigger button does not programmatically update aria-expanded to "true" when the menu opens. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state change to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside mobileNav.open function, after `_open = true;` add: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded update on close
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer trigger button does not programmatically update aria-expanded to "false" when the menu closes. Screen readers cannot announce the collapsed state change to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside mobileNav.close function, after `_open = false;` add: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks aria-controls pointing to the drawer element's ID. Screen readers cannot establish the programmatic relationship between the trigger and the controlled navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside mobileNav.init function, after `_drawer = qs(...);` add: `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, drawerId);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, keyboard focus is not trapped within the drawer. The _trap variable is declared but never initialized with createFocusTrap, allowing keyboard users to tab outside the modal into the background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside mobileNav.open function, after `_lockScroll();` add: `if (!_trap) { _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: false }); } _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing focus return on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, keyboard focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Users must manually navigate back to the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside mobileNav.close function, after `_unlockScroll();` and before emitting the event, add: `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded update on open
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded to "true" when their associated panel opens. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside dropdownMenu._openInstance function, after `inst.open = true;` add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded update on close
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded to "false" when their associated panel closes. Screen readers cannot announce the collapsed state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside dropdownMenu._closeInstance function, after `inst.open = false;` add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger missing aria-expanded update on open
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded to "true" when their answer panel expands. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside faqAccordion.open function, after `item.open = true;` add: `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger missing aria-expanded update on close
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded to "false" when their answer panel collapses. Screen readers cannot announce the collapsed state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside faqAccordion.close function, after `item.open = false;` add: `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded update on open
issuesFound.explanation: The search input combobox does not update aria-expanded to "true" when the suggestion list opens. Screen readers cannot announce that the listbox popup is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside siteSearch._open function, after `_searchState.open = true;` add: `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded update on close
issuesFound.explanation: The search input combobox does not update aria-expanded to "false" when the suggestion list closes. Screen readers cannot announce that the listbox popup is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside siteSearch._close function, after `_searchState.open = false;` add: `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) do not update aria-pressed to reflect the active pressed state. Screen readers cannot identify which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside pricing._syncToggleState forEach loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` add: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed to reflect the active filter state. Screen readers cannot identify which category filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside productFilter._syncTabAriaStates forEach loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` add: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are validated, the aria-invalid attribute is not set to reflect the validation state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the field contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside inlineValidator._renderResult, in the if (result.valid) block add: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` and in the else block add: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function that marks fields as invalid does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify the invalid state when this utility is used.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside srUtils.markInvalid function, after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` add: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function that marks fields as valid does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. The field may remain marked as invalid to screen readers after being corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside srUtils.markValid function, at the end add: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the product filter updates the visible count of results, the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed that the filter has been applied and how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside productFilter._updateCountBadge function, after `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', ...);` add: `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcement on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that the answer content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside faqAccordion.open function, after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });` add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : `${item.question} expanded`);` and similarly for close function with STRINGS.faqClosed.

issuesFound.title: pricing missing live region announcement on price change
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled and prices update, the price change is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that the displayed prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside pricing.setPeriod function, after `requestAnimationFrame(() => { _pricingState.animating = false; });` add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || `Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);`

fpTitles: mobileNav trigger missing focus return on close
srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
productFilter count badge missing live region announcement
faqAccordion missing live region announcement on toggle

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap created but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module instantiates a focus trap using createFocusTrap assigned to _trap, but the activate() method is never called when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the mobile navigation drawer into the background page content, breaking the modal dialog accessibility pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3865: Insert `if (_trap) _trap.activate();` immediately after `_lockScroll();` inside the open function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle buttons do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu show and hide dropdown panels using CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but they never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons. Screen reader users are not informed when dropdown menus open or close.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3954: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` at the end of the _openInstance function (before emitting the event).
  Line 3974: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` at the start of the _closeInstance function (before removing CSS classes).

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: During initialization, the search input is assigned role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox", but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false". Screen readers may incorrectly assume the listbox is expanded by default when the page loads.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4818: Insert `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after the line `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` in the init function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter category buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons (data-filter-value) indicate the active category filter but only update CSS classes in _syncTabAriaStates. They function as toggle buttons and require aria-pressed to convey the active state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5585: Inside the forEach loop in _syncTabAriaStates, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: breadcrumbHelpers missing aria-current on current page
issuesFound.explanation: The _processNav function in breadcrumbHelpers contains a comment indicating the last item should be marked as the current page, but the implementation code is missing. The last breadcrumb item lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen reader users from identifying their current location in the navigation hierarchy.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop module queries the scroll-to-top button from the DOM but does not ensure it has an accessible name via aria-label. If the HTML element lacks a label, screen reader users will hear only "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4125: Insert `if (!_btn.getAttribute(ARIA.LABEL)) setAttr(_btn, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top');` after the line `_btn = qs(SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn);` in the init function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button (_clearBtn) is queried from the DOM but the code does not ensure it has an aria-label. Screen reader users may not understand that this button clears the search input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4819: Insert `if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute(ARIA.LABEL)) setAttr(_clearBtn, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.labels.clearSearch || 'Clear search');` after querying the clear button.

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the _updateCountBadge function updates the visual product count badge text but does not announce the change via the liveRegion. Screen reader users are not informed how many products match the selected filters.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5555: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);` after the line `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', `${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`);` in _updateCountBadge.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods, the _updatePlanCard function updates prices visually and triggers animations, but the new price information is not announced to screen reader users. Users may not realize the displayed prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6135: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target} per ${period}`);` inside the _updatePlanCard function after the price display is updated (within the requestAnimationFrame or onComplete callback).

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion expand/collapse not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed via the open and close functions, the state changes are not announced via the liveRegion. Screen reader users may not be aware that content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 6855: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${item.title} expanded`);` at the end of the open function (before emitting the event).
  Line 6875: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${item.title} collapsed`);` at the start of the close function (after setting item.open = false).

issuesFound.title: contactForm personal data inputs missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields for full name, email, and other personal information do not have autocomplete attributes programmatically set. This prevents browsers from helping users autofill their information and fails WCAG 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 7725: After `const field = qs(cfg.selector, _formEl);` for the full_name selector, add `if (field) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`
  Line 7727: For the email selector, add `if (field) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`
  Line 7729: For other personal data fields (e.g., company), add appropriate autocomplete values such as 'organization'.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input (_emailEl) does not have autocomplete="email" programmatically set, failing WCAG 1.3.5 for identifying the input purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7615: Insert `if (_emailEl) _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');` after the line `_emailEl = qs('input[type="email"]', _formEl);` in the init function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but does not initialize aria-expanded to "false". Screen readers may assume the dropdown is open by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3938: Insert `if (!trigger.getAttribute(ARIA.EXPANDED)) setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in the register function after setting aria-haspopup.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher toggle buttons missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle buttons are updated with aria-pressed when set() is called, but on initialization, the buttons existing in the DOM do not have their aria-pressed state set to match the current theme.

fpTitles: scrollToTop button missing accessible name
contactForm personal data inputs missing autocomplete
newsletterForm email input missing autocomplete

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions manipulate the panel visibility but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded or collapsed state of the dropdown menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: In _openInstance(), after inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); Line 2: In _closeInstance(), after removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open); add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded state update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened, the function updates the visual state and animates the panel height, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Although the close function sets aria-expanded="false", the open function must set it to "true" to maintain the correct accessibility state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In faqAccordion.open(), after item.open = true; add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input toggles the visibility of the suggestion listbox, but it does not update aria-expanded to communicate the open or closed state of the popup to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: In _open(), after _searchState.open = true; add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); Line 2: In _close(), after _searchState.open = false; add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly, annual, etc.) function as toggle buttons that select one option from a set, but they only update CSS classes. They must use aria-pressed to indicate which period is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In _syncToggleState(), inside _periodBtns.forEach, after toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for filter results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates to show or hide cards, the change in result count is not announced to screen reader users. A live region announcement is required to inform users how many products are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In applyFilters(), after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches); add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products shown`);

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement for price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled, the price values update visually with animation, but screen reader users are not notified of the price change. A polite live region announcement should inform users of the new billing period and price.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In setPeriod(), after _updateSavingsMessage(period); add liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menu triggers are registered, they are not initialised with aria-expanded="false". Since the panels are initially hidden, the trigger must reflect this state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In dropdownMenu.register(), after panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is not initialised with aria-expanded="false". Since the suggestion listbox is initially closed, the input must communicate this state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In siteSearch.init(), after _initialised = true; add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus return on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the focus order and must navigate back to the trigger manually.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.close(), after _unlockScroll(); add if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is shown and hidden using CSS classes, but the aria-hidden attribute is not updated to match. When closed, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: In mobileNav.open(), after addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false'); Line 2: In mobileNav.close(), after removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons function as a tab list where one tab is active, but they only update CSS classes. They must use aria-selected to indicate the active tab to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In _syncTabAriaStates(), inside the forEach loop after toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); add setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button is not initialised with aria-pressed. When the carousel is set to auto-play, the button should reflect the playing state; when paused, it should reflect the paused state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In testimonialsCarousel.init(), after _initialised = true; and checking if _playBtn exists, add setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing)); where _playing is determined by the auto-play configuration.

fpTitles: mobileNav missing focus return on close

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure trigger button uses aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state of the submenu to screen reader users. This prevents users from knowing whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 850: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav accordion trigger button missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.fix: Line 1020: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component not exposing invalid state to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid, so screen readers cannot announce the error state of form fields.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button contains only an emoji icon and lacks an aria-label attribute, providing no accessible name for screen reader users. The IconButton interface requires aria-label but it is not provided at the call site.
issuesFound.fix:
<IconButton
    aria-label={LABELS[theme]}
    onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])}
    className="theme-toggle"
  >
    {ICONS[theme]}
  </IconButton>

    aria-busy={isLoading || undefined}

    {...rest}
  >

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element with role="dialog". This may cause assistive technology to incorrectly identify the modal boundary.

    <div
      ref={dialogRef}
      role="dialog"
      aria-modal="true"
      aria-labelledby={titleId}
      aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined}
      className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')}
      onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}
    >

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel hidden slides not marked aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Inactive slides in the carousel are visually hidden but remain accessible to assistive technology because they lack aria-hidden="true". This causes screen readers to read all slides instead of only the visible one.
issuesFound.fix:
<div
    key={t.id}
    role="group"
    aria-roledescription="slide"
    aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`}
    aria-hidden={!isVisible}
    className={[
      'testimonial-slide',
      isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : '',
    ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}
  >
